title: IOTM codegen: append new entries to iotms.ts
description: Build a CLI entrypoint that calls the scraper (see the scraper sub-issue) and appends any new IOTM records to packages/wishlist-shared/data/iotms.ts. Output must match the existing file's formatting so prettier is a no-op (key order matches the existing entries; trailing comma; 2-space indent; double-quoted strings). Approach options to evaluate: (a) string-templating new entries onto the file before the closing '];' marker — simple, low-risk; (b) AST manipulation via ts-morph or recast — heavier but safer if formatting drifts. Lean toward (a) for now. After write, the file should still parse and pass the existing tsconfig type-check.
acceptance_criteria: (1) Running the script with no new IOTMs is a no-op. (2) Running with a new IOTM appends the entry such that 'prettier --check' passes and 'tsc --noEmit' (via the existing tsconfig) still succeeds. (3) Idempotent: running twice in a row does not duplicate entries.

title: IOTM scraper: fetch and parse wiki + tier list data
description: Build a TypeScript module that, given no input (or an optional 'since' anchor), returns an array of IOTM records matching IOTMSchema for any IOTMs not yet present in iotms.ts. Sources: (a) KoL wiki — start from the Items_of_the_Month index page (https://kol.coldfront.net/thekolwiki/index.php/Items_of_the_Month) and per-item pages for ids/names/type/equipment_slot/img/tradeable plus opened_*/familiar_*/skill_* depending on type; (b) Google Sheets CSV exports of the speed and aftercore tier lists (sheet IDs in wiki/GLOSSARY.md) for tier values. is_ioty and is_con remain optional/manual — leave undefined unless trivially derivable. Module returns IOTM[] only; writing to disk is a separate concern (see the codegen sub-issue). Should be runnable in isolation for testing (e.g. 'bun run scrape -- --month=2026-04' prints JSON).
acceptance_criteria: (1) Calling the scraper for a known past IOTM produces a record byte-equivalent to its existing iotms.ts entry (modulo tier values if the sheet has been updated since). (2) Calling for a 'no new IOTM' state returns []. (3) Output validates against IOTMSchema.
close_reason: Implemented at packages/wishlist-shared/scripts/update-iotms/. 42 tests, all passing. Covers 33 historical IOTMs (every type) + 4 support pages.

title: Local script to update iotms.ts from wiki + tier lists
description: Automate the manual process of updating packages/wishlist-shared/data/iotms.ts when a new IOTM is released. Scope: a locally-runnable script (no scheduled CI, no PR automation yet). Sources: KoL wiki for item metadata (ids, names, type, equipment_slot, img, tradeable); community Google Sheets tier lists (URLs in wiki/GLOSSARY.md) for speed_tier/aftercore_tier. Manual fields like is_ioty/is_con stay manual. Two pieces: (1) a scraper that produces parsed IOTM records matching IOTMSchema in packages/wishlist-shared/schemas/data.ts; (2) a codegen step that appends new entries to iotms.ts while preserving prettier formatting.
acceptance_criteria: Running the script locally detects any IOTMs released since the last entry in iotms.ts, fetches their metadata + tier values, and appends well-formatted entries that pass IOTMSchema validation and existing prettier rules.
